briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 8265–8303 10th Avenue under newly adopted Bylaw #14780
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given final approval to Bylaw #14780, rezoning the properties at 8265–8303 10th Avenue. The new zoning rules for this land are now officially in effect.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property at 8265–8303 10th Avenue, review the new zoning rules with the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Burnaby shelter at 3020 Gilmore must submit Good Neighbour and Operations Plans before opening
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to block occupancy of the 3020 Gilmore shelter until BC Housing and the operator submit a Good Neighbour Plan and an Operational Management Plan that satisfy the City. Council also called on BC Housing to do more public consultation.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 3020 Gilmore, monitor City updates for the occupancy approval decision.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Procedure Bylaw amended — how meetings are run has changed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed all three readings of Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026, updating the rules that govern how Council meetings are conducted. The specific procedural changes were not detailed in the notice.

briefs.title: Burnaby now regulates bear spray sales under its Business Licence Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed all three readings of Bylaw #14798, adding bear spray sales as a regulated activity under the Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017. Businesses that sell bear spray in Burnaby must now comply with these new licensing rules.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business sells bear spray and confirm you meet Burnaby's new licensing requirements.
briefs.industries: Retail, Grocery & Food Retail, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Burnaby Bear Spray Bylaw Passes: New Rules May Affect Your Business
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed all three readings of an amendment to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, adding new rules around bear spray regulation. Businesses that sell or handle bear spray in Burnaby may now face new local compliance requirements.
briefs.action_required: Check with the City of Burnaby to confirm if your business sells or handles bear spray and what the new rules require.
briefs.industries: Retail, Grocery & Food Retail, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Burnaby plumbing permit fees updated — new costs apply to construction projects
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw #14800, which updates the fees charged for plumbing permits. If you pull plumbing permits in Burnaby, expect to pay the new rates going forward.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated Burnaby plumbing permit fee schedule before budgeting your next project.

briefs.title: Burnaby Rezones Two Buller Avenue Properties — Development Opportunities May Follow
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first two readings of a bylaw to rezone 7305 and 7317 Buller Avenue, moving the properties closer to a new land-use designation. Two more readings and final approval are still required before the rezoning is complete.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property near 7305–7317 Buller Ave, monitor future Council readings for final approval.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw 2026 approved — levy-paying businesses on North Road affected
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the Burnaby Business Improvement Area (North Road) Bylaw 2026, formally renewing or establishing the North Road BIA (a local business group funded by a mandatory levy on area businesses) for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary — you may owe a BIA levy in 2026.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek development permits may be paused while watershed protection rules are studied
briefs.summary: Council has sent a request to staff to study new watershed protection rules for Stoney Creek and consider pausing new development approvals in the area while that study is completed.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to develop near Stoney Creek, check with your local planning office before submitting any permit applications.

briefs.title: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Text Amendment Passes First and Second Reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has advanced Bylaw #14792, a text amendment (a written change to the rules) to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965. The bylaw has passed its first two of three required readings, moving it closer to becoming law.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's zoning rules if you own, lease, or develop property in Burnaby — this amendment may affect permitted uses.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower at Springer Avenue Advances Through First Rezoning Reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved first reading of a rezoning bylaw for a 49-storey residential and retail tower at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, including a housing agreement and a plan to sell City-owned land to the developer.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Springer Avenue, monitor future council readings for construction and zoning impacts.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City approves 2026 active transportation education and promotion initiatives
briefs.summary: City council has approved a set of education and promotion initiatives for active transportation (walking, cycling, and other non-motorized travel) planned for 2026, as recommended by the Transportation Committee.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek construction impact rules under feasibility review — changes may follow
briefs.summary: City council has sent recommendations from the Environment Committee about reducing construction disruptions near Stoney Creek to staff for a feasibility review. No new rules are in place yet, but a formal report is expected to follow.

briefs.title: 20-storey rental tower at 5058 Imperial Street replaces approved 6-storey plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw (a rule change that allows a different type of building on a property) for a 20-storey rental tower at 5058 Imperial Street, replacing a previously approved 6-storey plan.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor upcoming public hearings for this rezoning.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Village rezoning bylaw advances, allowing taller buildings and updated open space rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how land can be used or built on) for the Blake Village / Burnaby Lake Village area, updating building heights, massing (overall building size and shape), and open space requirements to align with the city's long-term plan, OCP 2050.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 6800 Lougheed Highway, monitor upcoming public hearings for this rezoning bylaw.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: On-Street Public Parking Fees Increased by City Council
briefs.summary: City council has approved an increase to on-street public pay parking rates. If you or your customers rely on street parking near your business, expect to pay more at the meter.
briefs.action_required: Check the new parking rates and inform customers who regularly use on-street parking near your business.

briefs.title: City-owned EV charging stations switch to energy-based fees under new bylaw amendments
briefs.summary: City council approved a new pricing model for public EV charging stations — fees will now be based on energy used (kilowatt-hours) instead of time parked. Several city bylaws are being updated to reflect this change.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business uses or manages City-owned EV chargers and review the new energy-based fee structure.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: City renews $162,400 portable toilet contract with Jiffy John Rentals
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Jiffy John Rentals Ltd. to supply portable toilets at an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes.

briefs.title: City renews $670,000 security contract with Guardteck for one more year
briefs.summary: City council approved a one-year renewal of its security services contract with Guardteck Security Corp, covering officer services at city buildings and special events, valued at approximately $670,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: Heritage Alteration Permit granted for chimney work at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a Heritage Alteration Permit (official permission to modify a protected historic building) for chimney alterations at the W.J. Mathers House 'Altnadene' at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Option B design for Landmark Burnaby Sign
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Option B design for the Landmark Burnaby Sign and directed staff to move forward with the project.

briefs.title: Burnaby approves up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard in 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake Park, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium, with noise bylaw exemptions covering March 15 to November 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to host or vend at outdoor events at these venues in 2026, confirm your event falls within the approved window.

key_dates.label: Burnaby noise bylaw outdoor event season begins — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard
Burnaby noise bylaw outdoor event season ends — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard

briefs.title: Antenna variance permit at 9001 University Crescent sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow larger antenna equipment on the building at 9001 University Crescent has been paused. City council sent it back to staff for more review before any decision is made.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Technology, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Commercial fleet parking permit renewed at 7000 Lougheed Highway
briefs.summary: Council approved the renewal of a Temporary Use Permit (a time-limited permission to use land in a way not normally allowed by zoning) for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals at 7000 Lougheed Highway.

briefs.title: Burnaby freezes $63,005 FCM membership dues until city gets Big City Mayors' Caucus seat
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has paused its 2026–2027 membership dues of $63,005 to the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) — the national group that represents cities — until Burnaby is given a seat on the Big City Mayors' Caucus.

briefs.title: Burnaby grants utility access easement over 3860 Sperling Avenue for Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a statutory right-of-way (a legal permission allowing utility companies to access and use a strip of private or city-owned land) over part of 3860 Sperling Avenue to connect utilities to the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre project.

briefs.title: Antenna variance application at 8888 University Drive sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow antenna equipment to exceed normal size and height limits at 8888 University Drive has been sent back to city staff for more review before a decision is made.

briefs.title: Burnaby now requires a special business licence to sell bear spray at retail
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council amended its Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to regulate the retail sale of bear spray. Stores that sell bear spray must now meet new licensing rules set by the City of Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Check with the City of Burnaby to confirm whether your store needs a new or updated licence to sell bear spray.

briefs.title: Construction permits paused in Lower Westridge pending natural hazard safety assessment
briefs.summary: City council launched a city-wide Natural Hazard Development Permit Area program and approved $120,000 to assess hazard risks in Lower Westridge. New construction permits in Lower Westridge are on hold until the assessment is complete.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to build or develop in Lower Westridge, expect permit delays until the hazard assessment is finished.

briefs.title: Burnaby plumbing permit fees updated — check new costs before your next project
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved small changes to plumbing permit fees in its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If you pull plumbing permits in Burnaby, the amount you pay may change once the updated bylaw is formally passed.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated Burnaby plumbing permit fee schedule before applying for your next permit.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA renewed for 2026–2031 with new bylaw coming
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a five-year renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA — a group that collects fees from local businesses to fund area improvements) and is preparing a new BIA Bylaw for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the North Road BIA zone — you may owe BIA fees starting 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw 2026 takes effect — renewal period begins

briefs.title: City will now remove outdated land restrictions from private properties on request
briefs.summary: The City approved the removal of old restrictive covenants (legal rules tied to a property that limit how it can be used) that it had previously placed on private land. Property owners can now request the City Solicitor to formally remove these outdated restrictions.
briefs.action_required: If your property has a City-imposed restrictive covenant, contact the City Solicitor's office to request its removal.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
